requirements.statement: The backend shall remain the authoritative source of runtime truth, command arbitration, and applied state publication.
The UI shall never silently imply authority that it does not possess.
Every signal shall have stable identity independent of user-facing display name.
Derived signals shall be dependency-tracked and cycle-checked before activation.
Output actions shall be requests subject to arbitration, permissives, interlocks, and safe-state policy rather than direct uncontrolled writes.
Requested, applied, and observed output states shall be distinguishable in runtime behavior and evidence surfaces.
Rules shall be represented by one canonical model with equivalent visual and DSL editing views.
Rule evaluation shall be explainable, including quality-blocked and indeterminate cases.
Modbus integration shall support TCP and RTU client modes with explicit decode, ordering, scaling, timeout, and health semantics.
Modbus-backed writable points shall participate in the same arbitration and authorization model as other outputs.
Sequences shall distinguish definition, revision, runtime instance, and execution state.
Sequence pause, hold, resume, timeout, abort, and recovery behavior shall be explicit and evidence-producing.
Historian data and event evidence shall preserve context linking runtime values, commands, alarms, rules, sequences, and diagnostics.
Live and historical review modes shall remain distinct and reversible without losing operator clarity.
Remote surfaces shall distinguish observation, supervision, and direct control capabilities, with backend-enforced boundaries.
Remote-issued commands shall be attributable and visible as remote-origin actions in local and remote evidence surfaces.
Alarm lifecycle shall distinguish event generation, alarm assertion, return-to-normal, latching, shelving, suppression, and acknowledgment.
Acknowledgment shall never erase historical alarm truth and shall remain attributable to actor, session, and time.
Diagnostics shall synthesize layered health across platform, backend, device, protocol, signal, output, and historian domains.
Watchdog behavior, heartbeat loss, degradation, and recovery shall be visible and evidence-producing.
Profiles, autosave, and restore shall preserve user/session continuity without silently implying restored live machine state.
Layout persistence and profile restore shall preserve workspace arrangements, subject to current live capability and authorization checks.
Device adapters shall provide a generalized abstraction layer above specific protocols while exposing capability, health, identity, and point metadata consistently.
Device-specific raw protocol detail shall remain available for service workflows without polluting ordinary operator workflows.
Exports and review bundles shall preserve provenance, baseline, timestamp, and covered evidence scope.
Review/export surfaces shall allow operators and reviewers to distinguish raw evidence from interpreted summaries.
Roles and permissions shall be backend-enforced, not merely UI-hidden.
Command authorization decisions shall remain attributable, reviewable, and linked to session, actor, and target action.
The shell shall preserve a graph-dominant operating posture with dockable control surfaces and recoverable workspace layout.
Workspace pages shall separate overview, devices, signals, outputs, rules, sequences, diagnostics, historian/review, and remote surfaces without ambiguity of purpose.
Graphing shall support live, review, and live-trace modes with explicit mode indicators and return-to-live behavior.
The graphing surface shall support event, alarm, command, rule, and sequence overlays where evidence relationships are relevant.
The rules editor shall provide deterministic roundtrip behavior between visual authoring and function-style DSL authoring.
Widget language shall distinguish live, stale, invalid, disconnected, simulated, blocked, pending, acknowledged, manual, sequence, rule, and remote states in a consistent manner.
Remote UI surfaces shall reflect remote capability limits and latency/connectivity truth explicitly rather than mimicking local parity when parity does not exist.
Every send-out package shall be auditable by the package-root audit tool and governed package audit specification.
Draft-residue/anti-pattern scanning shall use rule-scoped allowed-pattern logic by document class and shall not permit structural-integrity waivers.
The package-root master auditor shall perform structure, semantic, contradiction, and duplication audits for hardening packages.
Every release package shall declare package ID, code baseline ID, document baseline ID, compatibility statement, included artifacts, and change summary.
Completion claims shall be supported by explicit proof bundles rather than narrative assertion alone.
Controlled documents, requirement IDs, and cross-references shall remain machine-readable and internally consistent across the active corpus.
Every project-critical term shall have one canonical owner, declared alias policy, and governed anti-conflation treatment.
Material contradictions and semantic ambiguities shall be registered, resolved, or explicitly elevated before downstream implementation relies on them.
Meaningful duplication in normative content shall be classified as intentional, governed boilerplate, shadow definition, or consolidation-required before release-candidate use.
Future implementation packages shall be anchored to a governed file/module structure with declared boundary rules.

worked_examples.title: Requested command blocked by interlock before arbitration result is published
worked_examples.summary: A user requests an output change, authorization succeeds, an interlock denies apply, the request remains recorded, applied state is not advanced, and the rejection reason is persisted.

worked_examples.title: Requested command accepted, applied, then observed mismatch is surfaced
worked_examples.summary: A command request is accepted and applied, but observed feedback does not converge; the mismatch becomes explicit rather than silently collapsing applied and observed state.

worked_examples.title: Workspace restore rebuilds operator context without reasserting machine state
worked_examples.summary: A saved workspace restores page layout, graph selections, and trace visibility, while machine outputs and live authority remain untouched.

worked_examples.title: Alarm lifecycle assert acknowledge return-to-normal with historian evidence
worked_examples.summary: An alarm asserts, is acknowledged later, then returns to normal; all lifecycle transitions are timestamped and written to the evidence trail.

worked_examples.title: Graph mode separation between live review historical and live trace
worked_examples.summary: The operator can inspect historical data without presenting it as live, then re-enter live trace mode with explicit UI indication and no history/live conflation.

invariants.title: Requested applied and observed state shall remain distinct modeled concepts
invariants.expected_proof: state transition trace plus conformance assertion report

invariants.title: Restored profile and workspace state shall not be treated as live machine state
invariants.expected_proof: restore scenario trace, event log, no-write assertion

invariants.title: Graph live historical review and live-trace modes shall remain explicitly distinguishable
Authorization must precede command arbitration and apply publication
Restore workflows shall not reassert outputs as a side effect of UI or profile recovery
invariants.expected_proof: restore-origin event bundle with explicit no-output-write assertion

invariants.title: Alarm acknowledge cannot precede alarm assert
Return-to-normal cannot exist without prior asserted state
Stale degraded and disconnected timing thresholds shall remain distinct and explicit
Command timeout outcomes shall be explicit rather than silently abandoned
invariants.expected_proof: timeout event trace and outcome classification

invariants.title: Critical commands shall emit a durable event and historian evidence trail
invariants.expected_proof: proof bundle with event export and trace hash

invariants.title: Rejected commands shall record a concrete reason and origin
invariants.expected_proof: rejection reason event and audit export

invariants.title: Restore-originated actions shall be explicitly labeled as restore-originated
invariants.expected_proof: restore bundle with labeled origin chain

invariants.title: Alarm lifecycle changes shall reach historian evidence without silent omission
invariants.expected_proof: alarm historian export plus lifecycle trace

decisions.title: Execution contract exists before implementation code
decisions.summary: No feature implementation may be declared implementation-entry ready without an execution-contract entry derived from the active governed corpus.

decisions.title: First code slice shall be governance-aware baseline shell signal historian only
decisions.summary: The first code sprint is intentionally narrow and excludes output actuation, remote command execution, and full rules/sequences behavior.

decisions.title: Output actuation scope remains deferred until Sprint 2 closure
decisions.summary: The package still needs tighter execution-contract and invariant closure around arbitration, authorization, and observed mismatch proof before physical output code begins.

decisions.title: Remote supervision stays observation-only for first implementation entry
decisions.summary: Remote parity is still conceptually governed, but implementation entry remains blocked until command authorization and event evidence closure are complete.

decisions.title: Rules and sequences remain semantically closed but contract-light
decisions.summary: The semantics are mostly stable, but the next package should deepen invariant and worked-example coverage before these areas are treated as implementation-entry ready.

decisions.title: Executive summary becomes a required package artifact
decisions.summary: Every package revision shall include a root-level executive summary and a generated governance status report so a human can catch up quickly.
